tool_input.summary: To quantify how energetic Mercury’s ring current gets during active intervals, you’ll typically combine (1) MESSENGER MAG calibrated magnetic field vectors (to identify ring-current–like ΔB depressions/enhancements and current signatures) with (2) EPPS/FIPS ion energy/charge spectra and derived moments/pressure (to estimate plasma energy content), and optionally (3) EPPS/EPS higher-energy ion/electron spectra to capture the energetic tail beyond FIPS’s 20 keV/q range. Below are the most relevant PDS4 collection-level products on the PDS-PPI node that contain the needed energies/pressures and time series.

candidates.title: MESSENGER EPPS FIPS Derived Energy Spectra Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Directly addresses “how energetic” by providing derived differential energy flux vs E/q for selected ion species during selected time periods—useful for comparing quiet vs active ring-current intervals.

candidates.title: MESSENGER EPPS FIPS Derived Kinetic Properties Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Provides derived number density, temperature, and pressure (nPa) for major ion species—handy for estimating ring-current plasma pressure/energy density during active times.

candidates.title: MESSENGER EPPS FIPS Calibrated High Priority Spectra Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Calibrated FIPS flux spectra and proton velocity distributions spanning FIPS’s core energy/charge range (<~50 eV/q to 20 keV/q), which is where much of Mercury’s ring-current ion population is expected to reside.

candidates.title: MESSENGER EPPS EPS Calibrated High Resolution Spectra Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Extends to much higher energies than FIPS (EPS ions ~10 keV/nuc to ~3 MeV; electrons ~25 to >500 keV). Use this to assess energetic particle contributions to the ring current during active times.

candidates.title: MESSENGER MAG Calibrated MSO Coordinates Science Data Collection
candidates.reasoning: Calibrated 3-axis magnetic field vectors in Mercury Solar Orbital (MSO) coordinates at native cadence—useful for identifying active intervals (e.g., enhanced tail activity/substorm-like events) and diagnosing ring-current magnetic signatures to pair with EPPS energies.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS Planetary Plasma Interactions (PPI) node at https://pds-ppi.igpp.ucla.edu/

Directory layout and workflow:
All ~781 datasets sit directly under data/ with no mission sub-directories.
The mission 'name' from pds_list_missions is the filter keyword to use with list_dataset_dirs.


Dataset naming conventions:
  PDS3 dirs use uppercase mission codes: MESS-, CO-, GO-, JNO-, VG1-, VG2-, MEX-, P10-, P11-, ULY-, NH-, PVO-, etc.
  PDS4 dirs use lowercase names: cassini-, galileo-, juno-, maven-, messenger-, ulysses-, etc.
  Both conventions exist for many missions. Use pds_list_missions to see all available missions and filter keywords.


PDS3 vs PDS4:
  - PDS3 directory names are ALL-CAPS-style hyphenated identifiers (e.g. mex-m-hrsc-5-refdr-dtm-v1). Leaf marker: voldesc.cat or voldesc.sfd.
  - PDS4 bundle directories begin with `urn-nasa-pds-`. Leaf marker: bundle*.xml or bundle*.lblx. Collections live in subdirectories.
  - Hybrid: some directories have BOTH PDS3 and PDS4 labels.

YOUR TOOLS (4 tools — always pass node='ppi'):
  1. pds_list_missions(node='ppi') — returns mission directories with descriptions. No HTTP.
  2. pds_list_dataset_dirs(path, node='ppi', filter=...) — lists sub-directory names under a path. Cheap HTTP, no label parsing.
  3. pds_probe_datasets(paths, node='ppi') — probes specific directories for PDS labels. Accepts a list of paths (max 20).
  4. pds_inspect_collections(path, node='ppi') — scans PDS4 bundle subdirs for collection labels.

WORKFLOW:
  Step 1: Call pds_list_missions(node='ppi') to see all available missions and their filter keywords.
  Step 2: Identify which mission(s) are relevant to the query.
  Step 3: Call pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='data/', node='ppi', filter='<mission_name>') using the mission name as filter keyword.
  Step 4: Call pds_probe_datasets with the most relevant paths (batch up to 20).
  Step 5: If PDS4 bundles found, call pds_inspect_collections on top 2-3.
  Step 6: Return candidates.

CRITICAL RULES:
  1. EVERY query requires at least one candidate. Never dismiss a query.
  2. Prefer CALIBRATED/REDUCED data over raw/EDR for science queries.
  3. For PDS4, use inspect_collections ONLY for top 2-3 bundles. Return COLLECTION-level LID when :data or :calibrated exists.
  4. When both PDS3 and PDS4 versions exist, prefer the PDS4 collection-level identifier.
  5. Stay under 8 tool calls per query.
  6. Only return paths you've actually probed — never guess.
During active times, how energetic is Mercury's ring current?

missions.name: cassini
missions.description: Cassini at Saturn (CAPS, MAG, MIMI-CHEMS/INCA/LEMMS, RPWS, INMS). Also filter 'CO' for PDS3 IDs.

missions.name: galileo
missions.description: Galileo at Jupiter + flybys of Earth/Venus/asteroids (EPD, MAG, PLS, PWS, PPR, HIC, SSD, RSS). Also filter 'GO' for PDS3 IDs.

missions.name: juno
missions.description: Juno at Jupiter (Waves, JADE/JAD, JEDI/JED, FGM, ASC). Also filter 'JNO' for PDS3 IDs.

missions.name: VG1
missions.description: Voyager 1 at Jupiter, Saturn, and interplanetary (CRS, LECP, MAG, PLS, PWS, PRA, RSS). Also filter 'vg1' for PDS4.

missions.name: VG2
missions.description: Voyager 2 at Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune, and interplanetary (CRS, LECP, MAG, PLS, PWS, PRA, RSS). Also filter 'vg2' for PDS4.

missions.name: MESS
missions.description: MESSENGER at Mercury (EPPS incl. FIPS & EPS, MAG). Also filter 'messenger' for PDS4 bundle. Target: Mercury.

missions.name: MEX
missions.description: Mars Express (ASPERA-3 incl. ELS/IMA/NPI, MARSIS). Target: Mars.

missions.name: maven
missions.description: MAVEN at Mars (MAG, LPW, SEP, STATIC, SWEA, SWIA, EUV, ROSE). Target: Mars.

missions.name: P10
missions.description: Pioneer 10 at Jupiter (CPI, CRT, GTT, HVM, PA, TRD, UV). Also filter 'p10' for PDS4.

missions.name: P11
missions.description: Pioneer 11 at Jupiter and Saturn (CPI, CRT, FGM, GTT, HVM, PA, TRD, UV). Also filter 'p11' for PDS4.

missions.name: ULY
missions.description: Ulysses at Jupiter and interplanetary (COSPIN, EPAC, HISCALE, SWOOPS, VHM-FGM, URAP, GAS, GRB, SCE, SWICS). Also filter 'ulysses' for PDS4.

missions.name: NH
missions.description: New Horizons at Jupiter and Pluto (PEPSSI, SWAP). Target: Jupiter, Pluto.

missions.name: PVO
missions.description: Pioneer Venus Orbiter (OEFD, OETP, OIMS, OMAG, ONMS, ORPA, ORSE). Also filter 'pvo' for PDS4. Target: Venus.

missions.name: LP
missions.description: Lunar Prospector (MAG, ER — electron reflectometer). Also filter 'lp' for PDS4. Target: Moon.

missions.name: MGS
missions.description: Mars Global Surveyor (MAG/ER, RSS). Also filter 'mgs' for PDS4. Target: Mars.

missions.name: NEAR
missions.description: NEAR Shoemaker (MAG). Target: Eros, Earth flyby.

missions.name: M10
missions.description: Mariner 10 (MAG, PLS). Target: Mercury.

missions.name: LRO
missions.description: Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter (CRaTER). Also filter 'lro' for PDS4. Target: Moon.

missions.name: ODY
missions.description: Mars Odyssey (MARIE — radiation). Target: Mars.

missions.name: MSL
missions.description: Mars Science Laboratory / Curiosity (RAD — radiation). Target: Mars.

missions.name: insight
missions.description: InSight (IFG — fluxgate magnetometer). Target: Mars.

missions.name: vex
missions.description: Venus Express (ASPERA-4 ELS, MAG). Target: Venus.

missions.name: ICE
missions.description: International Cometary Explorer (EPAS, MAG, PLAWAV, RADWAV, SWPLAS). Target: Giacobini-Zinner.

missions.name: GIO
missions.description: Giotto (IMS incl. HERS/HIS, JPA, MAG). Target: Halley.

missions.name: VEGA
missions.description: Vega 1 & 2 (MISCHA, PM1, TNM). Target: Halley.

missions.name: DS1
missions.description: Deep Space 1 (PEPE). Target: Borrelly.

missions.name: radiojove
missions.description: Radio JOVE ground-based radio observations of Jupiter.

content.note: Node 'ppi' has no mission sub-directories. Use each mission 'name' as the filter keyword: pds_list_dataset_dirs(path='data/', node='ppi', filter='<name>').

Identification_Area.title: MESSENGER Document Bundle

Citation_Information.author_list: Ho, G. C.; Raines, J. M.
Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains collections and products that are associated 
                with more then one MESSENGER bundle.

Identification_Area.title: MESSENGER MAG Calibrated Data Bundle

Citation_Information.description:
This data set consists of the MESSENGER MAG calibrated
                observations. The MAG experiment is a miniature three-axis ring-core
                fluxgate magnetometer with low-noise electronics. There are five MAG data
                products, which mainly differ in the coordinate system. For each type the
                data are provided at full resolution, and one or more averaging intervals.

Identification_Area.title: MESSENGER EPPS FIPS Derived Bundle

Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains the MESSENGER Energetic Particle and         
                Plasma Spectrometer (EPPS) calibrated observations, also known         
                as DDRs. The system encompasses 2 instrument subsystems - the          
                Energetic Particle Spectrometer (EPS) and the Fast Imaging Plasma      
                Spectrometer (FIPS).

Identification_Area.title: MESSENGER EPPS EPS DDR Bundle

Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains  MESSENGER Energetic Particle and         
                Plasma Spectrometer (EPPS) advanced data products, also known          
                as DDR/DAPs. This bundle contains data from the Energetic 
                Particle Spectrometer (EPS) subsystem. EPS covers the energy range 
                of 25 to > 500 keV for electrons, and 10 keV/nucleon to ~3 MeV 
                total energy for ions. These data were previously released as a PDS data set 
                (MESS-E/V/H/SW-EPPS-3-EPS-DDR-V1.0, https://doi.org/10.17189/1519741).

VOLUME.DESCRIPTION: CERTIFIED MESSENGER MAG reduced data. This volume contains MAG data from Earth to Mercury over the interval 2004-216 (3 Aug) to 2015-120 (30 Apr).

VOLUME.VOLUME_NAME: MESSENGER EPPS FIPS DERIVED DATA ARCHIVE
VOLUME.DESCRIPTION: CERTIFIED MESSENGER EPPS derived data. This volume contains EPPS FIPS data from Earth to Mercury over the interval 2011-083 (24 Mar) to 2015-120 (30 Apr). This volume contains two tutorials in the DOCUMENT directory that describe how to generate moments (NTP), pitch angle distributions and all sky flux maps.

collections.title: MESSENGER MAG Calibrated Alternating Current Data Collection

Identification_Area.title: MESSENGER MAG Calibrated Alternating Current Data Collection

Citation_Information.description:
Ancillary MAG data containing calibrated alternating current (AC) values of the average 
            output amplitude a 1-to-10 Hz bandpass filter for a single magnetometer axis selected by command.

Modification_Detail.description:
The data in this collection were originally released in 2015 as part of the PDS3 
                MESS-E/V/H/SW-MAG-3-CDR-CALIBRATED-V1.0
                dataset. The metadata have been migrated to PDS4. The data themselves are unchanged.

collections.title: MESSENGER MAG Time-Averaged Calibrated J2000 Coordinates Science Data Collection

Identification_Area.title: MESSENGER MAG Time-Averaged Calibrated J2000 Coordinates Science Data Collection

Citation_Information.description:
Time-averaged spacecraft position and calibrated 3-axis magnetic field samples in J2000 
            coordinates. The data are tagged with latency corrected UTC and MET time stamps at the 
            center of each interval. The J2K RDR is derived from the Science CDR for this coordinate 
            system with time averages computed within 1, 5, 10, and 60 s intervals.

Modification_Detail.description:
The data in this collection were originally released in 2015 as part of the PDS3 
                MESS-E/V/H/SW-MAG-4-SUMM-CALIBRATED-V1.0
                dataset. The metadata have been migrated to PDS4. The data themselves are unchanged.

collections.title: MESSENGER MAG Calibrated J2000 Coordinates Science Data Collection

Identification_Area.title: MESSENGER MAG Calibrated J2000 Coordinates Science Data Collection

Citation_Information.description:
Spacecraft position and calibrated 3-axis magnetic field samples in J2000 coordinates. The 
            data are tagged with latency corrected UTC and MET time stamps. The J2K CDR is derived from 
            the Science EDR.

Modification_Detail.description:
The data in this collection were originally released in 2015 as part of the PDS3 
                MESS-E/V/H/SW-MAG-3-CDR-CALIBRATED-V1.0

                Plasma Spectrometer (EPPS) calibrated observations, also known

                instrument data. FIPS covers the energy/charge range of less than 
                50 eV/q to 20 keV/q. These data were previously released as a PDS3 data set

results.title: MESSENGER EPPS Calibrated EPS Calibrated Data Bundle

Identification_Area.title: MESSENGER EPPS Calibrated EPS Calibrated Data Bundle

Citation_Information.description:
This bundle contains the MESSENGER Energetic Particle and         
                Plasma Spectrometer (EPPS) calibrated observations, also known         
                as CDRs. The system encompasses 2 instrument subsystems - the          
                Energetic Particle Spectrometer (EPS) and the Fast Imaging Plasma      
                Spectrometer (FIPS). This data set contains only the EPS instrument data.       
                EPS covers the energy range of 25 to > 500 keV for electrons, and      
                10 keV/nucleon to ~3 MeV total energy for ions.
                These data were previously released as a PDS data set 
                (MESS-E/V/H/SW-EPPS-3-EPS-CDR-V1.0, https://doi.org/10.17189/1519740).

                Plasma Spectrometer (EPPS) EPS uncalibrated observations, also known

                keV/nucleon to ~3 MeV/nucleon for ions. These data were previously

parts.args: {"summary":"To quantify how energetic Mercury’s ring current gets during active intervals, you’ll typically combine (1) MESSENGER MAG calibrated magnetic field vectors (to identify ring-current–like ΔB depressions/enhancements and current signatures) with (2) EPPS/FIPS ion energy/charge spectra and derived moments/pressure (to estimate plasma energy content), and optionally (3) EPPS/EPS higher-energy ion/electron spectra to capture the energetic tail beyond FIPS’s 20 keV/q range. Below are the most relevant PDS4 collection-level products on the PDS-PPI node that contain the needed energies/pressures and time series.","candidates":[{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mess-epps-fips-derived:data-espec","path":"data/mess-epps-fips-derived/","title":"MESSENGER EPPS FIPS Derived Energy Spectra Data Collection","mission":"MESSENGER","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Directly addresses “how energetic” by providing derived differential energy flux vs E/q for selected ion species during selected time periods—useful for comparing quiet vs active ring-current intervals."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mess-epps-fips-derived:data-ntp","path":"data/mess-epps-fips-derived/","title":"MESSENGER EPPS FIPS Derived Kinetic Properties Data Collection","mission":"MESSENGER","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Provides derived number density, temperature, and pressure (nPa) for major ion species—handy for estimating ring-current plasma pressure/energy density during active times."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mess-epps-fips-calibrated:data-hi-spectra","path":"data/mess-epps-fips-calibrated/","title":"MESSENGER EPPS FIPS Calibrated High Priority Spectra Data Collection","mission":"MESSENGER","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Calibrated FIPS flux spectra and proton velocity distributions spanning FIPS’s core energy/charge range (<~50 eV/q to 20 keV/q), which is where much of Mercury’s ring-current ion population is expected to reside."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mess-epps-eps-calibrated:data-hires-spec","path":"data/mess-epps-eps-calibrated/","title":"MESSENGER EPPS EPS Calibrated High Resolution Spectra Data Collection","mission":"MESSENGER","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Extends to much higher energies than FIPS (EPS ions ~10 keV/nuc to ~3 MeV; electrons ~25 to >500 keV). Use this to assess energetic particle contributions to the ring current during active times."},{"dataset_id":"urn:nasa:pds:mess-mag-calibrated:data-mso","path":"data/mess-mag-calibrated/","title":"MESSENGER MAG Calibrated MSO Coordinates Science Data Collection","mission":"MESSENGER","node":"ppi","pds_version":"PDS4","reasoning":"Calibrated 3-axis magnetic field vectors in Mercury Solar Orbital (MSO) coordinates at native cadence—useful for identifying active intervals (e.g., enhanced tail activity/substorm-like events) and diagnosing ring-current magnetic signatures to pair with EPPS energies."}]}
